question_text: Sound is a longitudinal wave that requires a medium to travel through. Which of the following best explains why you cannot hear sound in the vacuum of space?

options.label: A
options.text: There are no sound waves produced in space

options.label: B
options.text: Sound waves cannot travel through the empty space because there are no particles to compress and stretch

options.label: C
options.text: Sound waves are too weak to reach Earth from space

options.label: D
options.text: The human ear cannot detect sounds that have traveled very far

explanation: Sound is a longitudinal wave where particles in a medium vibrate back and forth, creating compressions and rarefactions that travel outward. In a vacuum, there are no particles to vibrate, so sound cannot be transmitted. This is why astronauts in space need radios to communicate.
